description_long: This gatehouse features a traditional East Asian architectural style, with a prominent arched entryway framed by thick, whitewashed stone walls that show signs of weathering and age. The roof is composed of multiple tiers with upturned eaves, covered in brown tiles, and extends outward in three distinct sections, giving the structure a grand and imposing appearance. The base of the gatehouse is reinforced with dark stone blocks, adding to its sense of solidity and permanence. Above the archway, a blue sign with Chinese characters is mounted, serving as a decorative and possibly informative element. The side views reveal the depth of the structure and the layered roof design, while the rear mirrors the front but with less ornamentation. The overall appearance suggests a historic or ceremonial function, likely serving as an entrance to a significant urban or cultural site.
description: A weathered East Asian gatehouse with a multi-tiered, upturned tile roof, arched stone entryway, and decorative blue signage above the entrance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched entryway, blue sign with Chinese characters, and the full three-tiered roof structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the gatehouse, layered roof eaves, and weathered wall textures.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the arched opening and roof tiers but lacking the blue sign and with more visible wall wear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same layered roof and wall details as the other side, with less visible weathering.
attribute_entryway_design: arched stone entryway with decorative blue signage above
